title: Pitcairnia pusilla
taxonomy: Pitcairnia pusilla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It belongs to the genus Pitcairnia, which includes a large number of tropical plants. This plant is native to Central and South America, and its classification as a bromeliad places it within the order Poales. The family Bromeliaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Pitcairnia pusilla falling under the subfamily Pitcairnioideae. Within the plant kingdom, Pitcairnia pusilla is a monocot, characterized by having one cotyledon during germination.
uses: Pitcairnia pusilla is known to have various medicinal properties, although further research is required to confirm its effectiveness.
